executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims on June 16th across 18 different ransomware gangs. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (clothing retailer), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, Kids & Company (childcare), and Eeyou Communications Network (telecom). The Qilin gang leads with 18 victims, followed by Play with 11, and Clop with 13. Multiple critical sectors are affected: public health, education, financial services, construction, manufacturing, and municipal government. No specific CVEs or official CCCS advisories were reported in this window.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a massive ransomware attack wave on June 16th with 101 organizations publicly listed as victims by 18 different ransomware gangs. Critical infrastructure targets include public health (Peterborough Public Health), municipal government (North Perth), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed database leak). No CCCS advisories were published in this 48-hour window.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (clothing retailer, hit by CoinbaseCartel), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail, hit by Sinobi)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi), Trucash (Lynx), multiple financial services including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Reidhurst Nagy

report_2w.executive_summary: The U.S. government issued an unprecedented national security directive on June 12, 2026, forcing Anthropic to immediately suspend worldwide access to its most advanced AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, over unspecified security concerns. A critical supply chain attack compromised the npm ecosystem with malicious packages including 'atomic-lockfile', affecting the Axios library and hundreds of Arch Linux AUR packages—this represents active exploitation of the JavaScript/Node.js supply chain. The threat actor ShinyHunters exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273) to breach multiple universities. Additionally, the AudiA6 cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs was disrupted by Europol, and multiple ransomware groups including Qilin and Payload continue active campaigns.

items.description: Zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft exploited by ShinyHunters to breach universities
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: SolarWinds Serv-U vulnerability added to CISA KEV catalog, denial of service vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Low, Integrity: Low, Availability: High

items.description: Critical Open Redirect in Aqara Cloud OAuth allowing phishing and session theft, no patch available
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.description: Critical vulnerability in nezhahq nezha Monitoring allowing RoleMember users to inject OS commands on all servers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Denial of service vulnerability in VMware vCenter
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: None, Integrity: None, Availability: High

items.description: Apache vulnerability being actively scanned by Chinese IP addresses
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: ShinyHunters (UNC6240)
items.targets: Multiple universities
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - exploited Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day CVE-2026-35273

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

items.targets: Swiss organizations
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: NPM supply chain attackers

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Shadowbyt3$ group

items.ttps: T1598 (Phishing for Information), T1583 (Acquire Infrastructure)

items.name: Chinese threat actor (L'Encodeur de Shanghai)
items.targets: Multiple targets scanning for Apache vulnerabilities
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - CVE-2021-41773

items.name: AudiA6 crypto laundering service (disrupted)
items.targets: Ransomware payment processing
items.ttps: T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information), T1521 (Encrypted Channel)

canada_landscape.summary: No specific CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. Multiple articles reference Canada in general contexts including World Cup 2026 hosting, political news, and general cybersecurity discussions, but no direct cyber incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure or major organizations were reported. The npm supply chain compromise and ransomware activity affecting global targets would impact Canadian organizations using these technologies.
